claim: QwQ (Qwen with Questions) is a local reasoning model but has significant flaws: excessive looping, random Chinese output, and chain-of-thought outputs that include internal thoughts making it hard to extract final answers
actionable: If using QwQ locally, implement post-processing or prompt chaining to extract final answers from reasoning tokens rather than using outputs directly
source_title: Yup, QwQ is CRACKED: Prompt Chaining with Qwen and QwQ reasoning model

claim: Prompt chaining pattern: use a reasoner model (QwQ) to generate rich contextual outputs, then use a base model (O1 Mini, Claude) to extract clean, actionable results
actionable: Chain QwQ output through an extraction model to remove reasoning tokens and get structured final results
source_title: Yup, QwQ is CRACKED: Prompt Chaining with Qwen and QwQ reasoning model

claim: Running prompt chains through local models (QwQ) then cloud models (O1 Mini, GPT-4o) is faster overall due to cloud model speed despite local model latency
actionable: Don't assume local-only chains are fastest; test cloud-then-local chains for reasoning + extraction workflows
source_title: Yup, QwQ is CRACKED: Prompt Chaining with Qwen and QwQ reasoning model

claim: Prompt chaining pattern is ultra-useful for cleaning outputs from reasoning models that will become mainstream in 2026 (e.g., OpenAI o1 with visible reasoning)
actionable: Build prompt extraction patterns now to prepare for future reasoning models where chain-of-thought is visible output rather than hidden
source_title: Yup, QwQ is CRACKED: Prompt Chaining with Qwen and QwQ reasoning model

claim: Local reasoning models like QwQ make advanced reasoning capabilities available on-device but are still worth using despite slowness and language mixing issues
actionable: Evaluate local reasoning models (QwQ) for use cases where cloud-only reasoning is unfeasible or too expensive, accepting current limitations
source_title: Yup, QwQ is CRACKED: Prompt Chaining with Qwen and QwQ reasoning model
